content:
Blockchain is a secure way to record transactions without relying on a central bank or authority. Instead of one single entity controlling the process, blockchain uses a network of computers around the world. When a transaction occurs, it is grouped with others into a “block.” This block is then shared with every participant in the network, who work together to verify and agree that the transaction is valid. Once confirmed, the block is added to a chain of previous transactions, creating a permanent and unchangeable record.

Because the transaction history is distributed across many computers, it is nearly impossible to alter or counterfeit. This decentralized system builds trust without needing a bank or middleman. For busy executives, this means faster, more transparent, and cost-effective transactions that can improve business efficiency and security. Blockchain’s design removes the need for a central authority while ensuring every transaction is recorded accurately and securely.
